rag_rules: Place data constraint assumptions in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports with concrete bounds.
Use AGREE constants for explicit numeric thresholds in assumptions and guarantees.
Generate boolean range expressions using visible variables with compatible types.
Avoid inserting assumptions for ports without explicit numeric bounds or types.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be syntactically valid and scoped to appropriate AADL component types or implementations.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the data types of referenced variables.
Do not assume numeric constraints unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants in AGREE are declared with `const` keyword and must have explicit types.
Guarantees relate outputs to inputs or other visible expressions using logical operators.
Assumptions must be placed in valid component type scopes for port references.
anti_patterns: Do not generate assumptions for ports without explicit numeric bounds or types.
Do not assume non-negative values unless explicitly required by the requirement.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean ports into integer comparisons without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Model fusion and scope merging rules
